amendments.body: Finish Issue 3's new-row backfill path. `src/adapters/fs.rs:3041-3059` still creates fresh `TaskRunEntry` rows with `plan_hash: None` whenever the caller omits it, even when `snapshot_plan_hash()` returned the milestone's canonical hash. That leaves newly started lineage rows without plan lineage, which is the exact behavior this milestone was supposed to remove.
amendments.rationale: This is not just a missing optimization: every start recorded without an explicit `plan_hash` still persists incomplete lineage data. The test at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5185-5242` currently asserts the wrong behavior, so the suite will keep passing until the adapter-side backfill is implemented and the expectation is flipped.

amendments.body: Finish Issue 3's existing-open-row repair path. `src/adapters/fs.rs:2570-2623` intentionally makes `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` return `false` whenever `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None`, so open/legacy rows with `plan_hash: None` can never be repaired from the milestone snapshot when they are revisited through `record_task_run_start()` or `update_task_run()`.
amendments.rationale: That leaves the accepted backfill fix incomplete for exactly the recovery case it was meant to cover: older in-flight lineage rows remain permanently detached from the canonical plan hash. The test at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5392-5475` currently locks in this deviation.

payload.summary: Reviewed the lineage/journal changes in `src/adapters/fs.rs` and the new regression coverage in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`. The new runless-to-named terminal-replay fall-through logic looks correct and the targeted replay tests pass, but Issue 3 is still intentionally unimplemented in two places: fresh rows still skip snapshot `plan_hash` backfill, and existing open rows with missing provenance still never recover a `plan_hash`.
